_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So `selectedByGroup` is created as `new Map<string, string>()` or `new Map([...])` with `[x.name, x.now]` which are strings. So the Map should be `Map<string, string>`. And `Map.prototype.get` for `Map<string, string>` returns `string | undefined`.

So why would TypeScript infer the return type as `unknown`? Perhaps the issue is that when you use `.map((x: { name: string; now: string }) => [x.name, x.now])`, TypeScript might infer the Map as `Map<string, string>` but in some strict mode or with a different version it could be inferring the tuple as something that makes the Map's value type unknown.

Actually wait - when you do `new Map([["a", "b"], ["c", "d"]])`, TypeScript infers that as `Map<string, string>`. So `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)` should be `string | undefined`.

Unless - perhaps the `.map()` returns something that TypeScript sees as `[string, string][]` but the Map constructor might be inferring a wider type. I will check - `new Map(iterable)` where iterable is `[string, string][]` - the Map would be `Map<string, string>`. So get returns `string | undefined`.

The fix could be to add an explicit return type to the useCallback:
```ts
const getSelectedForGroup = useCallback(
  (groupName: string): string | undefined => selectedByGroup.get(groupName),
  [selectedByGroup],
);
```

That should fix the error by explicitly annotating the return type as `string | undefined` instead of letting TypeScript infer `unknown`.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_


Adding an explicit return type so TypeScript infers `string | undefined` instead of `unknown` for `getSelectedForGroup`.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Good, no linter errors. The fix was to add an explicit return type `: string | undefined` to the callback so it matches the expected type `(groupName: string) => string | undefined` in ProxyRender's props. The issue was that TypeScript was inferring the return type of `selectedByGroup.get(groupName)` as `unknown` (possibly due to the Map being inferred from the useMemo in a way that lost the value type), so the explicit annotation resolves the error.

_**Agent (default)**_


The error came from `getSelectedForGroup` being inferred as `(groupName: string) => unknown` while `ProxyRender` expects `(groupName: string) => string | undefined`.

An explicit return type was added so the callback matches that signature:

```ts
(groupName: string): string | undefined => selectedByGroup.get(groupName)
```

`selectedByGroup` is a `Map<string, string>`, so `.get(groupName)` is really `string | undefined`; the annotation makes that clear to TypeScript and removes the TS2322 error. You can run `pnpm run web:build` again to confirm the build passes.
